text:
The Beauty of Bunaken National Park

Bunaken National Park is a world-famous marine park located at the northern tip of Sulawesi Island, Indonesia. Established in 1991, it covers an area of approximately 890 square kilometers. The park is renowned for its incredibly rich biodiversity, particularly its underwater life. It is home to over 390 species of coral and thousands of fish species, making it a paradise for divers and snorkelers from all around the globe.

The most striking feature of Bunaken is its massive underwater walls, also known as "hanging gardens." These vertical coral walls drop deep into the ocean, covered in vibrant sponges, sea fans, and soft corals. The water is exceptionally clear, with visibility reaching up to 30 meters on a good day. This clarity allows visitors to see the intricate details of the reef and the schools of colorful fish, such as butterflyfish, damselfish, and the occasional sea turtle swimming gracefully by.

On the islands themselves, the environment is equally beautiful. Lush mangroves fringe the coastline, providing a habitat for various birds and crabs. The local community is very involved in preserving the park, working alongside the government to reduce plastic waste and protect the reefs from destructive fishing practices.

Visiting Bunaken is not just about enjoying the view; it is an education in the importance of marine conservation.
q: What is the primary reason Bunaken National Park is famous among international visitors?
options: Its large land area for hiking
The variety of rare birds in the mangroves
Its rich underwater biodiversity and coral species
The government buildings located on the islands
Its proximity to the northern tip of Sumatra

text:
My Helpful Robot, "Bimo"

I have a unique assistant at home named Bimo. Bimo is a small, round vacuum robot that helps my family keep the house clean. He is about 30 centimeters in diameter and has a sleek, metallic silver finish. On his top surface, there is a single glowing blue button that serves as his power switch.

Bimo is equipped with advanced sensors that allow him to navigate around furniture without bumping into things. When he encounters a wall or a chair leg, he gently slows down and changes direction. He is also smart enough to detect stairs, so he never falls down. My favorite feature is his "auto-charge" function; when his battery is low, he automatically finds his way back to his small charging dock in the corner of the living room.

Although Bimo is just a machine, we treat him like a pet. He makes a cheerful "beep" sound when he finishes cleaning a room. He is very quiet, so I can study while he works on the floor. Bimo saves us a lot of time and energy every day.
q: Categorize each statement based on the text into Fact or Opinion.
items: Bimo has a diameter of about 30 centimeters.
Bimo is the most adorable robot ever made.
Bimo returns to his dock when his battery is low.
Bimo makes a beep sound after cleaning.

text:
A Memorable Trip to Borobudur Temple

Last summer, my family and I decided to visit Borobudur Temple in Magelang. We left our house in Yogyakarta at 4:30 AM to catch the sunrise. The air was chilly, but we were very excited. When we arrived, we had to climb hundreds of steep stone stairs to reach the top. As the sun began to rise, the sky turned into a beautiful mix of orange and purple. The view of the surrounding mountains and the morning mist was breathtaking. I took many photos of the stupas and the intricate relief carvings on the walls.

After spending two hours exploring the temple, we went down and had a traditional breakfast at a nearby local stall. Although my legs were sore from climbing, the experience was truly unforgettable.
q: What was the main reason the writer's family left very early in the morning?
options: To avoid the heavy traffic in Magelang
To witness the sunrise from the temple
To have breakfast at a local stall
To clean the stupas before other tourists arrived
To avoid the cold weather in Yogyakarta

text:
The Proclamation of Indonesian Independence

The Proclamation of Indonesian Independence was a turning point in the nation's history. It occurred on Friday, August 17, 1945. The event was prompted by the surrender of Japan to the Allied forces after the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki. Earlier that morning, a group of young activists (the "Pemuda") urged Soekarno and Hatta to declare independence immediately. After intense discussions, the proclamation text was drafted at the house of Rear Admiral Maeda.

At 10:00 AM, Soekarno, accompanied by Moh. Hatta, read the short declaration in front of his house at Jalan Pegangsaan Timur No. 56, Jakarta. The red-and-white flag, sewn by Mrs. Fatmawati, was hoisted, and the national anthem "Indonesia Raya" was sung. This simple yet solemn ceremony marked the birth of a new sovereign nation, free from colonial rule.

items: The proclamation took place on August 17, 1945.
The flag was sewn by Mrs. Fatmawati.
The ceremony was the most beautiful event in the world.
The text was drafted at Rear Admiral Maeda's house.

text:
The Weaver and the Golden Thread

In a small village tucked between two purple mountains, there lived a young weaver named Elara. Elara was known for her steady hands and her ability to create the softest blankets in the land. However, she was never satisfied. She dreamed of weaving a fabric that could capture the light of the moon.

One evening, while she was working late, a tiny spider with silver legs crawled onto her loom. Instead of brushing it away, Elara watched as the spider spun a web that shimmered like diamonds. "If only I had thread as bright as your silk," Elara sighed. To her surprise, the spider spoke in a voice like clicking needles. "I will give you a spool of golden thread," the spider said, "but you must promise to weave a garment for someone who has nothing, rather than selling it for gold." Elara greedily agreed, thinking only of the fame such a dress would bring her.

The next morning, a spool of glowing thread appeared. Elara worked feverishly for three days, crafting a gown so beautiful it blinded the eyes. She planned to show it to the King. However, as she finished the last stitch, the gold turned into dry straw. The gown became heavy and ugly. Confused and crying, Elara remembered the spider's warning.

Just then, an old, shivering woman passed by her window. Elara felt a sudden pang of guilt. She took the straw gown, which was still warm, and wrapped it around the woman’s shoulders. The moment the fabric touched the beggar, it transformed back into radiant gold, warming the woman instantly. Elara finally understood that the magic of the thread was not in its color, but in the kindness of the weaver’s heart.
q: What was Elara’s main ambition at the beginning of the story?
options: To become the richest person in the purple mountains
To learn how to speak to magical insects
To create a fabric that looked like moonlight
To travel to the King's palace and live there
To build a larger house for her family

text:
The Secret of the Whispering Woods

Leo was not an adventurous boy, but when his younger sister, Mia, disappeared into the Whispering Woods, he had no choice but to follow. The woods were famous for their strange echoes and trees that seemed to move when no one was looking. Armed only with a dim flashlight and a wooden stick, Leo stepped onto the mossy path.

text:
The Clockmaker’s Daughter

In the clock-tower city of Oakhaven, time was the most valuable thing. Every citizen was given a small silver watch at birth that showed how much time they had to complete their life's work. Clara, the daughter of the Master Clockmaker, was different. Her watch didn't tick; it stayed perfectly still at twelve o'clock. Because of this, many people in the city thought she was cursed or broken.

Clara spent her days in her father’s workshop, surrounded by cogs, springs, and the smell of oil. One day, the Great City Clock—the machine that powered all the watches in Oakhaven—suddenly stopped. The city fell into a panic. People froze in place, and the sky turned a dull grey. Only Clara could still move, because her time had never been tied to the Great Clock. She realized that the "curse" was actually a gift.

She climbed the highest tower and looked into the giant gears of the Great Clock. She found that a small bird had built a nest inside the main spring, stopping the mechanism. Clara gently moved the nest to a safe window ledge and used her own "still" watch to jump-start the gears. As she touched the main wheel, her watch finally began to tick. The city roared back to life, and the sky turned blue again.

The people realized that being "different" didn't mean being "broken." Clara wasn't a girl with no time; she was the girl who held time in her hands.
q: Categorize each statement based on the text into Fact or Opinion.
items: Every citizen in Oakhaven receives a silver watch.
Clara is the most talented girl in the city.
The Great City Clock stopped because of a bird's nest.
Clara is the daughter of the Master Clockmaker.

text:
The Critical Importance of Adolescent Sleep

Quality sleep is often overlooked in the busy lives of teenagers, yet it is a fundamental pillar of health and development. While many adolescents view staying up late as a rite of passage or a necessity for studying, research consistently shows that sleep deprivation can have severe consequences. It is essential that both parents and educators prioritize sleep hygiene for the younger generation.

Firstly, sleep is vital for brain development and academic performance. During deep sleep, the brain processes the information gathered throughout the day, consolidating memories and strengthening neural connections. A sleep-deprived student often struggles with concentration, problem-solving, and creativity. Consequently, students who maintain a consistent sleep schedule of 8 to 10 hours tend to perform significantly better in examinations compared to those who sacrifice sleep for "cramming."

Secondly, sleep plays a crucial role in emotional regulation. Adolescence is already a period of significant hormonal changes and emotional volatility. Lack of sleep exacerbates these issues, leading to increased irritability, anxiety, and even depression. A well-rested teenager is better equipped to handle social pressures and daily stressors with a balanced perspective.

Furthermore, physical health is directly linked to sleep duration. During sleep, the body releases growth hormones and repairs tissues. It also strengthens the immune system, making the body more resilient against common illnesses. Chronic sleep loss in teenagers has been linked to long-term health risks, including obesity and cardiovascular problems.

In conclusion, sleep is not a luxury but a biological necessity. By understanding the academic, emotional, and physical benefits of rest, society must work together to ensure that teenagers are given the time and environment they need to sleep adequately.

options: To describe the different stages of the human sleep cycle.
To criticize schools for giving students too much homework.
To persuade the reader that teenagers need sufficient sleep for their overall well-being.
To explain the biological chemical reactions that occur during dreaming.
To compare the sleep habits of teenagers in the past with those of today.

text:
The Environmental Case for Remote Work

The global shift toward remote work, accelerated by recent technological advancements, has proven to be more than just a matter of convenience. Beyond its impact on productivity, remote work offers significant environmental benefits. Transitioning to a "work-from-home" model is a strategic move that can contribute to a more sustainable future.

The most immediate environmental advantage is the reduction in carbon emissions. Daily commuting is a major contributor to air pollution and greenhouse gas emissions. When employees work from home, the number of cars on the road decreases significantly. This reduction in traffic leads to lower fuel consumption and a noticeable improvement in urban air quality. Furthermore, decreasing the demand for public transport and personal vehicles reduces the overall carbon footprint of the workforce.

In addition to reduced emissions, remote work leads to lower energy consumption in commercial buildings. Large office complexes require vast amounts of electricity for lighting, heating, cooling, and operating electronic equipment. When offices are at lower capacity or closed, the energy demand drops. While home energy use might increase slightly, it rarely matches the intensive energy requirements of maintaining a large-scale corporate facility.

Moreover, remote work encourages a "paperless" culture. In a traditional office setting, the reliance on printed documents is often high. However, remote teams naturally rely on digital tools, cloud-based sharing, and electronic signatures. This shift significantly reduces paper waste and, consequently, the environmental impact of the logging and paper manufacturing industries.

In conclusion, remote work is a powerful tool for environmental conservation. By reducing emissions, saving energy, and minimizing waste, it aligns corporate interests with global sustainability goals.

options: Difficulty with concentration
Enhanced creativity and problem-solving
Increased irritability and anxiety
Better performance in examinations
Struggles with memory consolidation

text:
The Necessity of Financial Literacy in Schools

As students transition into adulthood, they face a complex world of credit cards, loans, and investment opportunities. Unfortunately, many young people enter the "real world" without basic financial knowledge. Therefore, it is imperative that financial literacy becomes a mandatory part of the high school curriculum.

One primary reason is the prevention of debt. Many young adults fall into the trap of high-interest credit card debt because they do not understand how interest rates or minimum payments work. By teaching students how to manage debt and understand credit scores, schools can help them avoid financial burdens that could last for decades.

Furthermore, financial literacy empowers students to plan for the future. Understanding the power of compound interest and the importance of early savings can lead to long-term wealth and stability. Students who learn to budget their money are more likely to achieve their goals, such as buying a home or starting a business, without relying on predatory loans.

Finally, financial education protects individuals from fraud. In the digital age, financial scams are becoming increasingly sophisticated. A financially literate person is better at recognizing "too good to be true" offers and protecting their personal information. Knowledge is the best defense against those who wish to exploit financial ignorance.

In summary, providing students with financial skills is an investment in their future. It ensures they are responsible, prepared, and protected as they navigate their economic lives.

items: Financial literacy is the most important subject in the world.
Compound interest and early savings can lead to long-term stability.
Financial scams have become increasingly sophisticated in the digital age.

text:
The Necessity of Financial Literacy in Schools

As students transition into adulthood, they face a complex world of credit cards, loans, and investment opportunities. Unfortunately, many young people enter the "real world" without basic financial knowledge. Therefore, it is imperative that financial literacy becomes a mandatory part of the high school curriculum.

One primary reason is the prevention of debt. Many young adults fall into the trap of high-interest credit card debt because they do not understand how interest rates or minimum payments work. By teaching students how to manage debt and understand credit scores, schools can help them avoid financial burdens that could last for decades.

Furthermore, financial literacy empowers students to plan for the future. Understanding the power of compound interest and the importance of early savings can lead to long-term wealth and stability. Students who learn to budget their money are more likely to achieve their goals, such as buying a home or starting a business, without relying on predatory loans.

Finally, financial education protects individuals from fraud. In the digital age, financial scams are becoming increasingly sophisticated. A financially literate person is better at recognizing "too good to be true" offers and protecting their personal information. Knowledge is the best defense against those who wish to exploit financial ignorance.

In summary, providing students with financial skills is an investment in their future. It ensures they are responsible, prepared, and protected as they navigate their economic lives.
